Sion Base
Street
Chemin Lambien 12
Location
1950 - SION
Country
Switzerland
Infrastructure
NrRoomFurnishing DescriptionWorkplacesSize (m²)
1Hangar1 Hangar Spot 19 m × 19 m × 7.50 m (height)1 Super Puma360
2HESLO gear RoomStorage space for equipment 2 m × 1.5 mStorage5
3OfficePhone, Fax, Computers, Printer, Toilets, Shower—10 / 20
4KitchenA well-equipped kitchen, fridge, Dish washer, Oven—22
5Meeting Room4 m × 7 m—28
6Parking SpotOutside vehicle1 car—
Access — Helicopter Arrival
Procedure to follow
  • Before flight: call Alpark at +41 79 108 52 28
  • Provide your estimated time of arrival (ETA)
  • Once landed, remain with the helicopter and wait for an Alpark representative to meet you
Refuelling: When making your phone call, request refuelling if required. If possible, our advice would be to refuel directly upon arrival in order to save time before the next flight.
Sion International Airport
  • ICAO: LSGS
  • IATA: SIR
  • Position: N46°13.2' E007°19.6'
  • Elevation: 1580 ft  ·  Variation: E0°24.0'
  • Fire Category: 3 up to 7 on request
  • Slots: Sion Airport does not have slot restrictions
  • Operating hours: 7 days a week / 365 days a year
  • April–September: 07.00 LT – 20.00 LT
  • October–March: 08.00 LT – SUNSET
  • Contact: contact@alpark.ch
Runways
RWYMH / CRSDimensionsSurfaceApproach Aids
07MH 072°2000 m × 40 mAsphaltPAPI 3.0° • ALSF-1
25MH 252°2000 m × 40 mAsphaltILS 110.70 MHz • HDG 246° • PAPI 3.5° • ALSF-1
Radio & Navigation Frequencies
  • Sion Ground: 121.705 MHz
  • Sion Tower: 118.275 MHz
  • ATIS: 130.630 MHz  (Voice service: +41 (0)22 417 40 80)
  • Airport Handling: 131.475 MHz
  • Sion Approach: 126.825 MHz
  • ILS RWY 25: 110.70 MHz
  • VOR SION: 112.15 MHz
Ground Arrival
  • Drive up to the main gate
  • Ring the intercom to alert ALPARK duty staff
  • Identify yourself when requested via the intercom
  • Follow the instructions provided by the ALPARK staff
  • Park in the designated parking area
  • Proceed to the assigned entrance door (H7 or H8)
Important remarks
  • Comply at all times with safety and security instructions provided by ALPARK staff.
  • Report immediately any abnormalities or safety concerns to ALPARK duty personnel.

Hangar

Miscellaneous
Miscellaneous
Albertville Base
Street
Route de l'aérodrome
Location
73460 TOURNON
Country
France
Infrastructure
  • Floor area: 507 m²  ·  Type of floor: Vibrated concrete
  • Heating: Oil-fired (forced air)
  • Lighting: Neon
  • Openings: 3 sliding doors
  • Fire Safety: hand-held fire extinguishers
  • Equipment: 2-tonne hoist; compressed air; 380 V and 220 V AC electrical terminals
  • Storage: shelves, main blade racks
  • Office: 50 m²
Access — Helicopter Arrival
Procedure to follow
  • No procedures are required for arrival or departure.
Refuelling: Fuel distribution is carried out using the SAF Hélicoptères badge. The refueling operator must note the helicopter's registration number on the paper form located in the pump room.
Albertville Airport
  • ICAO: LFKA
  • IATA: XAV
  • Position: N45 37 35 E006 19 44
  • Elevation: 1035 ft
  • Fire Category: Level 1
  • Contact: GEMILIS AERO — Aérodrome d'Albertville — 73460 Tournon
  • Tel: +33 4 79 10 89 01
  • E-mail: bureaudepisteLFKA@gemilisaero.com
Runways
RWYQFUDimensionsSurfaceProcedures and special instructions
05049800 m × 21 mAsphaltPreferred RWY 23: valley breeze. TKOF RWY 23: first turn at 1700 ft AMSL minimum. TKOF RWY 05: as soon as possible, left turn and climb North sector. Helicopter approach RWY 05 or 23: aim for runway center.
23229800 m × 21 mAsphalt—
Radio & Navigation Frequencies
  • Air/Air: 123.500 MHz
Ground Arrival
  • Drive up to H3 hangar
  • Open the key box — Code: 4524
Important remarks
  • Comply at all times with safety and security instructions provided by ALPARK staff.
  • Report immediately any abnormalities or safety concerns to ALPARK duty personnel.

LEASING OVERVIEW BY FOCA
APP05-OMM · Leasing Overview by FOCA
APP05-OMMVer 3.001.01.2026
Leasing

The requirements for the different kinds of leases are described in the following tables.

Always check both sides: first, where does the aircraft come from, and second, where does it go to (e.g. it may come from an « AOC-dry-lease-out » and go to a « SPO-dry-lease-in »).
Contents
  • 1   Leasing definitions / checklist
  • 2   SPO-leasing with Swiss or European Operators (EASA Member states)
  • 3   SPO-leasing with Third Country Operators (TCO)
1 — Leasing definitions / checklist
Dry-lease-inDry-lease-outWet-lease-inWet-lease-out
DefinitionsAdd aircraft to SPO-Declaration of SAF InternationalRemove aircraft from SPO Declaration of SAF InternationalOther AOC/SPO operator flies for SAF InternationalSAF International S.A. flies for another AOC/SPO operator
Operating banNo leasing with an aircraft or an operator which is subject to an operating ban according to EC 2111/2005 will be undertaken.
HB- registry for a foreign ownerTo register in the Swiss aircraft registry, the owner must be a Swiss citizen or a Swiss company. Except when the aircraft is going to be used by a Swiss AOC or SPO Operator, then this operator may apply for an exemption. An application (incl. the leasing agreement) for this special authorization acc. to art. 3 par. 2 (LFV SR748.01) will be sent to FOCA / Registry Office.
Leasing responsibilityThe Accountable Manager (ACM) is responsible for any leasing and shall sign the leasing agreement and the leasing statement.
Leasing agreementDescription of all the lease provisions between owner and operator of the aircraft, incl. names and addresses, registration, type and serial number and duration — except financial arrangements. ORO.AOC.110(d).
Leasing statementWritten confirmation by the ACM that all the parties to the lease agreement fully understand their respective responsibilities under the applicable regulations. ORO.AOC.110.
InsuranceThe insurance must be amended to cover the whole leasing operations.
ICAO 83bis SurveillanceIt is up to the State of Registry to decide whether it wants to delegate its oversight responsibilities to the state of the operations. This procedure, described in article 83bis of ICAO Doc 10059, is complex and may be very time consuming. Therefore, the civil aviation authorities must be informed by the operators as soon as possible in order to perform the arrangements before the planned start of the leasing.
Foreign aircraft customsFor aircraft that are not registered in Switzerland: check with the Swiss customs authority (BAZG / OFDF) which formalities apply.
Foreign national lawsIf the flights take place outside of Switzerland, check the applicable foreign national laws to apply them. Eventually some specific authorizations are needed. For example: customs / tax, off-airport landings, flying under the minimum altitude, flight time limitations (FTL), others…
VBR I / ORE IVerordnung über die Betriebsregeln im gewerbsmässigen Luftverkehr — Ordonnance sur les règles d'exploitations du trafic aérien commercial.
Abbreviations used
Dry-lease-inDry-lease-outWet-lease-inWet-lease-out
DefinitionsAdd aircraft to SPO-Declaration of SAF InternationalRemove aircraft from SPO Declaration of SAF InternationalOther AOC/SPO operator flies for SAF InternationalSAF International S.A. flies for another AOC/SPO operator
FTLFlight Time Limitations
AOC OpsSpecsAir Operator Certificate / Operations Specifications
SPAOperation requiring Specific Approval
OMOperation Manual (OMM, OM-A, OM-B, OM-C, OM-D)
SPO / SOPSpecialized operations / Standard Operating Procedures
HRAHigh Risk Authorization for commercial specific operations
MOUSwiss Mountain authorization for pilot
CAMO / CAMEContinuing Airworthiness Management Organization / Exposition
C of ACertificate of Airworthiness
AMPAircraft Maintenance Program
MELMinimum Equipment List
2 — SPO-leasing with Swiss or European Operators (EASA Member states)
Dry-lease-inDry-lease-outWet-lease-inWet-lease-out
DefinitionsAdd aircraft to SPO-Declaration of SAF InternationalRemove aircraft from SPO Declaration of SAF InternationalOther AOC/SPO operator flies for SAF InternationalSAF International S.A. flies for another AOC/SPO operator
No FOCA leasing-approvalNo approval of the leasing is required for any leasing between commercial SPO-operators having both their principal place of business in an EASA member state (GM1 ORO.SPO.100(c)).
Cross-border high-risk commercial SPO is requiredAdd aircraft. Send to FOCA: new SPO-declaration; apply for a new high-risk authorization; send changes to SPA, SOPs, OMs, MEL, AMP, CAME (whatever applies).Delete aircraft. Send to FOCA: new SPO-declaration; apply for a new high-risk authorization; send changes to SPA, SOPs, OMs, MEL, AMP, CAME (whatever applies).Check if the foreign SPO Operator has valid (applicable to the type of desired SOP): C of A, CAMO, insurances, SOP, high-risk SPO authorization for Switzerland (if not, he must apply for it, to its own Civil Aviation Authority). Request for: flying under the minimum altitude, off-airport landing / MOU. Check that: FTL acc. VBR I; all personnel have a working permit for Switzerland.Application to FOCA for a cross-border high-risk authorization valid in the desired country (incl. SOP and risk assessment). Check if there are other national requirements in that country.
End of leasingRemove aircraft: reverse all changes from above.Add aircraft: reverse all changes from above.N/AN/A
3 — SPO-leasing with Third Country Operators (TCO)
Dry-lease-inDry-lease-outWet-lease-inWet-lease-out
DefinitionsAdd aircraft to SPO-Declaration of SAF InternationalRemove aircraft from SPO Declaration of SAF InternationalOther AOC/SPO operator flies for SAF InternationalSAF International S.A. flies for another AOC/SPO operator
FOCA Leasing approvalDry & wet lease-in require an approval from FOCA prior to operations (ORO.SPO.100(c)). Dry & wet lease-out requires the approval of the TCO country.
Application for Leasing-approval and Cross-border high-risk commercial SPOApplication incl.: provide evidence that non-EU aircraft is available; maximum duration 7 months within any 12 consecutive months; show compliance with EU 1321/2014; aircraft is equipped according to EASA Part-SPO; leasing agreement; leasing statement; valid C of A. Addition of aircraft: new SPO-declaration; apply for a new high-risk authorization; send changes to SPA, SOPs, OMs, MEL, AMP, CAME (whatever applies).Delete aircraft from SPO-declaration and high-risk authorization. Send to FOCA: new SPO-declaration; apply for a new high-risk authorization; send changes to SPA, SOPs, OMs, MEL, AMP, CAME (whatever applies). Apply the requirements of the TCO country.Application incl.: maximum duration 7 months within any 12 consecutive months; show compliance with EU 1321/2014; C of A acc. ICAO Annex 8; leasing agreement; leasing statement; demonstrate equivalence to Part-SPO, Part-ORO, Part-SPA, Part-CAMO, Part-145 (for more details see AMC1 ORO.SPO.100(c)(1)); request for flying under the minimum altitude; request for off-airport landing / MOU. Check that: FTL acc. VBR I; all personnel have a working permit for Switzerland. A record of all TCO wet-lease-in must be maintained for inspections by FOCA. For short-term TCO wet-lease-in, a framework agreement may be used (GM1 ORO.AOC.110(c)).Apply the requirements of the TCO country.

SAFETY PROMOTION
APP07-OMM · Training & Awareness — ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
APP07-OMMVer 3.001.01.2026
Regulatory basis: ORO.GEN.200(a)(6) requires a structured safety promotion sub-pillar. EASA distinguishes initial / recurrent / advanced (NP) training (SM, NP, pilots, technicians) along with safety culture measurement tools via safety surveys.
1 — SMS Training Register

Traceability of personnel trained in SMS, including the selected training type, training date, automatic validity date two years later, and an alarm when the remaining validity is less than three months. Per GM1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6) and AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6).

Click any cell to edit, including Valid until and Alarm · Each generated certificate is automatically added/updated here · Validity is calculated at +2 years · Reference: SMS-YYYY-NN, based on the training year and automatically incremented · Alarm: Valid / < 3 months / Expired / Planned
NameRole / FunctionTraining type Organisation / TrainerTraining dateValid until ReferenceAlarm
Xavier BOUVET SM / CMM Initial training EASA / SAF International 01.01.2024 01.01.2026 — Valid
Thierry BERGERET NPCT / NPFO / BM Advanced training (NP) EASA / SAF International 01.01.2024 01.01.2026 — Valid
Sacha CARRERE NPGO Advanced training (NP) EASA / SAF International 01.01.2024 01.01.2026 — Valid
Julien METRO NPCA Advanced training (NP) EASA / SAF International 01.01.2024 01.01.2026 — Valid
— To be completed — Technician Initial training SAF International — — — Planned
2 — Training Certificate Generator

Generate an A4 landscape training certificate using the uploaded SMS - Training certificate background.
The candidate name is printed after I certify that:. The training type is printed below the completed-training sentence, and the selected TM08/TM09 module is printed two lines below it. The certificate date is printed to the right of the Date: label, on the same line. The Safety Manager name is not printed because it is already included in the background with the signature.

✏️ Complete the fields, then click Preview / Print. The trained person is added automatically to the editable register.
⚡ Fill from training register:
3 — Safety Surveys / Culture Perception Questionnaires

Tool for capturing staff safety perception — explicitly required by GM1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6). Used to measure the evolution of safety culture and identify weak signals not captured by the reporting system.

Objective: Measure staff perception on safety culture, reporting quality, management responsiveness — and track trends over time (SMS trend indicator).
✏️ Click to edit · Campaign results are summarised in the "Summary" column
CampaignDateScope RespondentsResponse rateOverall score /10 Summary / Key points
SUR-2024-01 01.06.2024 All SAF CH staff — — — Initial campaign — to be planned and deployed
Recommended Topics (GM1 ORO.GEN.200)
  • Reporting culture: does staff feel comfortable reporting an incident without fear of sanction?
  • Just Culture: is the Just Culture policy known and perceived as genuine?
  • Safety communication: does safety information flow effectively down to the field?
  • Management responsiveness: are corrective actions resulting from reports visible and explained?
  • Workload / pressure: do operational pressures compromise safety?
  • SMS knowledge: does staff know the SMS procedures, contacts and reporting channels?
Recommended Deployment Plan
StepActionResponsibleDeadline
1Draft questionnaire (10–15 Likert questions)SMQ3 2026
2Validation by ACM and NPsACM + NPQ3 2026
3Deployment (paper or anonymous online form)SMQ4 2026
4Analysis of results + SM reportSMQ4 2026
5Presentation at SRB — corrective actions if neededSM + ACMQ4 2026
6Renew annually (SPI trend measurement)SMAnnual
Tip: choose "Landscape" · Margins = None
